title: tetramolopium arenarium subsp. arenarium
intro: Tetramolopium arenarium subsp. arenarium, also known as the Sandy Tetramolopium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The Sandy Tetramolopium has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
